name: Japanese Novel
comment: Instant (pre-activation) colorization for .jpnov. Scope families are chosen so VS Code's DEFAULT semantic-type→scope fallback lands on the SAME colors the LSP semantic layer later emits (comment→comment, keyword→keyword — see src/server/semanticTokens.ts HIGHLIGHTS): when the server's tokens arrive they refine invisibly instead of repainting. Every pattern is a single-line `match` (never begin/end) so an unclosed ［＃ or a runaway 《 can only ever mis-color its own line. The 傍点 variant alternation must stay in sync with src/shared/compiler/emphasis.ts DOTS.

patterns.comment: Page break ［＃改ページ］ — frame greyed, directive keyword-colored (semantic: marker/directive).
Emphasis span END ［＃(の左に|左に)?<dot>終わり］ — must be tried BEFORE span start (終わり suffix).
patterns.match: (［＃)(の左に|左に)?(白ゴマ傍点|二重丸傍点|蛇の目傍点|黒三角傍点|白三角傍点|白丸傍点|丸傍点|ばつ傍点|×傍点|傍点)(終わり)(］)
(［＃)(の左に|左に)?(白ゴマ傍点|二重丸傍点|蛇の目傍点|黒三角傍点|白三角傍点|白丸傍点|丸傍点|ばつ傍点|×傍点|傍点)(］)

patterns.comment: Postfix emphasis ［＃「対象」(に|の)?(の左に|左に)?<dot>］ — the target (group 3) is deliberately UNSCOPED so it keeps the default foreground, exactly like the semantic layer. The target must be NON-empty ([^」]+): the tokenizer rejects an empty target (comment fallback), so an empty one must fall through to the generic annotation rule below.
patterns.match: (［＃)(「)([^」]+)(」)(に|の)?(の左に|左に)?(白ゴマ傍点|二重丸傍点|蛇の目傍点|黒三角傍点|白三角傍点|白丸傍点|丸傍点|ばつ傍点|×傍点|傍点)(］)

patterns.comment: Any other ［＃…］ (block layout, 見出し, the 傍線 line family, unknown) — whole annotation greyed, matching the server's `comment` classification. Single-line, so an unclosed ［＃ never runs away.
patterns.name: comment.block.annotation.novel-jp

patterns.comment: Ruby reading 《…》 incl. both brackets — greyed like the semantic marker; the base (親文字) before it keeps the default foreground. Empty 《》 / a lone 《 are left default (the tokenizer treats them literally).
patterns.name: comment.other.ruby.novel-jp

patterns.comment: Explicit ruby base marker ｜ — the 親文字 after it stays default.
patterns.name: comment.other.ruby.novel-jp

patterns.comment: Dialogue corner-bracket delimiters — greyed; the utterance content keeps the default foreground (the semantic layer masks it too). Matching per CHARACTER keeps multi-line 「…」 stateless and safe.
patterns.name: comment.other.dialogue.novel-jp
